name: User
description: This object represents a Telegram user or bot.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this user or bot. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this identifier.

fields.name: is_bot
fields.description: True, if this user is a bot

fields.name: language_code
fields.description: IETF language tag of the user's language

fields.name: is_premium
fields.description: True, if this user is a Telegram Premium user

fields.name: added_to_attachment_menu
fields.description: True, if this user added the bot to the attachment menu

fields.name: can_join_groups
fields.description: True, if the bot can be invited to groups.

fields.name: can_read_all_group_messages
fields.description: True, if privacy mode is disabled for the bot.

fields.name: supports_guest_queries
fields.description: True, if the bot supports guest queries from chats it is not a member of.

fields.name: supports_inline_queries
fields.description: True, if the bot supports inline queries.

fields.name: can_connect_to_business
fields.description: True, if the bot can be connected to a user account to manage it.

fields.name: has_main_web_app
fields.description: True, if the bot has a main Web App.

fields.name: has_topics_enabled
fields.description: True, if the bot has forum topic mode enabled in private chats.

fields.name: allows_users_to_create_topics
fields.description: True, if the bot allows users to create and delete topics in private chats.

fields.name: can_manage_bots
fields.description: True, if other bots can be created to be controlled by the bot.
